insulator.notes: Reference anchor only. Do not copy blindly; infer the actual target from the specific material, cell size, dimensionality, and accuracy tier.

metal.notes: Reference anchor only. Metals often need denser sampling than insulators, but do not generalize one metal to all others.

topological.notes: Reference anchor only. Topological materials vary strongly with SOC, dimensionality, and layer character.

semiconductor.notes: Reference anchor only. Use the GaAs primitive-cell semiconductor scale as a category anchor, but still infer the final answer from the specific material and cell.

optical.notes: Reference anchor only. Optical targets can span semiconductors and insulators; use the actual material physics, not category label alone.

ferroelectric.notes: Reference anchor only. Ferroelectrics can have larger cells and softer distortions; do not transfer small-cell sampling blindly.

piezoelectric.notes: Reference anchor only. Use anisotropic sampling when the cell shape and reciprocal-space resolution justify it.

magnetic.notes: Reference anchor only. Magnetic materials vary strongly with metallicity and correlation.

thermoelectric.notes: Reference anchor only. Layered thermoelectrics often need anisotropic meshes; do not copy isotropic anchors blindly.

superconductor.notes: Reference anchor only. Superconductors vary strongly with metallicity, SOC, and dimensionality.
